Send server-side web, app, and offline c: Send server-side web, app, and offline conversion events to Pinterest using the Conversions API.
The Conversion access token generated in: The Conversion access token generated in Ads Manager (Conversions → Conversions API).
The numeric ID of the ad account that ow: The numeric ID of the ad account that owns these conversions.
\nSend server-side conversion events to:
Send server-side conversion events to Pinterest with the **Conversions API**.

**1. Get your Conversion Access Token**
1. Open **Pinterest Ads Manager**.
2. Go to **Ad Account Overview → Conversions → Conversions API → Set up API**.
3. Choose **Conversion access token** and click **Generate new token**.
4. Copy the token and paste it below.

**2. Find your Ad Account ID**
It is the numeric ID shown in Ads Manager (also in the URL as `advertiserId`).

Your token's user must own the ad account or have an Admin, Analyst, Audience, or Campaign role via Business Access.
Send a single web, app, or offline conve: Send a single web, app, or offline conversion event to Pinterest via the Conversions API.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint
The type of conversion that occurred.
Required when Event Name is "Custom". Use 1–100 letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens.
Unix timestamp in seconds of when the ev: Unix timestamp in seconds of when the event happened. Leave empty to use the current time.
Unique ID used to deduplicate this event: Unique ID used to deduplicate this event against the Pinterest tag. Use the same value on both sides. Leave empty to generate one automatically.
The URL where a web conversion happened.
Whether the user opted out of tracking /: Whether the user opted out of tracking / ad personalization for this event.
### Customer Information\nProvide at lea:
### Customer Information
Provide at least **one** of: Email, Mobile Advertising ID, or both Client IP Address **and** Client User Agent. Personal identifiers are automatically normalized and SHA-256 hashed before sending — enter the raw values.
Customer phone number including country: Customer phone number including country code. Hashed automatically (symbols and spaces are stripped).
Numeric ZIP code. Pinterest accepts digi: Numeric ZIP code. Pinterest accepts digits only; symbols and spaces are removed before hashing.
Your own unique identifier for the user: Your own unique identifier for the user (e.g. loyalty or account ID). Hashed automatically.
The user's GAID (Android) or IDFA (iOS). Hashed automatically.
The user's IP address (IPv4 or IPv6). Se: The user's IP address (IPv4 or IPv6). Sent unhashed.
The user's browser user-agent string. Se: The user's browser user-agent string. Sent unhashed.
The value of the _epik cookie or the epi: The value of the _epik cookie or the epik query parameter from the landing URL.
ISO-4217 currency code (e.g. \"USD\"). D: ISO-4217 currency code (e.g. "USD"). Defaults to the ad account currency.
Total value of the event (e.g. order tot: Total value of the event (e.g. order total). Recommend pre-tax, pre-shipping.
Product IDs associated with the event.
Total number of products in the event.
Helps Pinterest deduplicate events. Reco: Helps Pinterest deduplicate events. Recommended for checkouts.
The search term for a search event.
For third parties sending on behalf of a: For third parties sending on behalf of an advertiser. Lowercase "ss-partnername".
When enabled, the event is validated but: When enabled, the event is validated but NOT recorded. Use to verify your setup, then turn off for live events.
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
